meaning: n.(土地等的)使用和占有,(官职等的)保有,任期,(土地)使用期限
examples: It's still extremely difficult to get tenure.
I know they will continue to provide valuable guidance as I begin my tenure as an international civil servant.
There can be no question that throughout Delmont's tenure the average paycheck in this city has been getting steadily bigger.
Prof Beddington made his comments in the final week of his tenure as the government's chief scientific adviser.
Muto's short-lived tenure as the Fox "mole" wasn't particularly well thought-out in the first place.
